questions.question: In Rama's opinion, the weight of her son Amit is not more than 72 kg. In Amit's opinion, his weight is not greater than 74 kg but not less than 66 kg. According to Amit's brother, Amit's weight is not less than 67 kg but not more than 70 kg. If each of them are correct in their estimation, then what is the average of different possible weights of Amit (in kg)?
questions.explanation: Let the weight of Amit be W. According to Rama, W ≤ 72 According to Amit, 66 ≤ W ≤ 74 According to Amit's brother, 67 ≤ W ≤ 70 Now, the value satisfying all the conditions are 67, 68, 69 and 70. Therefore the average of different possible weights of Amit is (67 + 68 + 69 + 70)/4 = 68.5 kg

questions.question: What should be added to 8x³-12x²+2x+5 to make it exactly divisible by (2x+1) ?
questions.explanation: If the given equation is exactly divisible by (2x + 1) 2x+1→x=-½ Use the remainder theorem to find the remainder: f(-½)=8(-½)³-12(-½)²+2(-½)+5 f(-½)=8(-⅛)-12(¼)+2(-½)+5=-1-3-1+5=0

questions.question: The diagonal of the square is 5√2 cm. The diagonal of the other square whose area is double that of the first?
questions.explanation: diagonal of square= √2 a a= side of square. now, d = 5√2 Side of the square = 5 cm Area of the square = 5 * 5 = 25 cm² Area of other square of double the area = 2 * 25 = 50 cm² Side of square = √50 = 5√2 cm Diagonal of the square = 10 cm

questions.question: Find the speed of the man walking in the direction of the train if the length of train is 1000m and is moving with a speed of 66km/hr and it takes 60sec for the train to cross the man.
questions.explanation: Relative speed of train = Relative distance /relative time => Relative speed of train = 1000/60 => Relative speed of train = 50/3m/sec => Relative speed of train = 50*18 /3*5 => Relative speed of train = 60 km/h => Actual speed of the train - speed of the man = relative speed of the train => 66 - x = 60 => x = 6km/hr

questions.question: An equilateral triangle is such that P and Q are mid-points of sides AB and AC and PQ is parallel to BC. If length of PQ is 10 cm, then what is the length of BC? (in cm)
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p1260/image004.png) => Since P and Q are mid points AB and AC and PQ || BC => Therefore, AP/AB =AQ/AC = PQ/BC => ½=PQ/BC => BC=2*10 => BC = 20cm

questions.question: Find the value of (x⁴ +5x³ + 7 x² +5x + 1) / (x⁴ + 1) if x + 1/x =2.
questions.explanation: => x+ 1/x = 2 Squaring on both sides we get => x² + 1/x² + 2=4 => x² + 1/x² = 2 => x⁴ + 5x³ + 7x² + 5x + 1 / x⁴ + 1 => Dividing each term by x² => x² + 5x + 7 + 5/x + 1/x² / x² + 1/x² => Now x² + 1/x² = 2 => (x² + 1/x²) + 5(x+1/x²) + 7 / x² + 1/x² => 2+ 5*2 + 7 / 2 => 2+ 10+ 7 / 2 => = 19/2 => = 9.5

questions.question: The price of a commodity is Rs 2000 and the seller want to earn a profit of 8%.If the seller lists the commodity at 20% above the price, find what should be the allowed discount.
questions.explanation: Short cut method : => Let the discount allowed be d => 20 - d - 20x/100=8 => 12=120d/100 => d= 10% Alternative method: => SP=(1+8/100)*2000 => Rs.2160 => And list price =(1+20/100)*2000= Rs.2400 => Discount=2400 - 2160 => Rs.240 => Discount %=240*100/2400 => 10%

questions.question: A shopkeeper purchased an article by paying 10% less than original price and he then sells it at 25% profit on the price that he had purchased. Calculate the profit percentage earned by the shopkeeper on the original price.
questions.explanation: => Let original price = Rs 100 => Price paid by him = 100-10 = 90 => And S.P = 90*125/100 => 112.50 => Profit % on original price i.e Rs 100 is => (112.5 - 100)*100/100 % => 12.5 %

questions.question: A and B can complete a work in 7 days, B and C in 21 days and C and A in 28 days. Find in how many days will A complete the work alone?
questions.explanation: => (A+B)'s 1 day's work=⅐ => (B+C)'s 1 day's work= 1/21 => (C+A)'s 1 day's work= 1/28 => 2 =⅐+1/21+1/28 => =(12+4+3)/84 => =19/84 => (A+B+C) 1 day's work =19/2*84=19/168 => A's 1 day work =(A+B+C) 1 day's work -(B+C)'s 1 day's work = 19/168 - 1/21 => =(19-8)/168 => 11/168 => A will complete the work in 168/11 days.

questions.question: Find the surface area of the cube which in formed by melting three smaller cubes of sides 12 cm, 20 cm, and 16cm.
questions.explanation: => Total volume of 3 cubes = (12)³ + (20)³ + (16)³ => 1728 + 8000 + 4036 => 13824 cm ³ => New case volume = a³ => a³ = 13824 => a = 24 => Surface area = 6a² => 6*24*24 => 3456 cm²

questions.question: In an right-angle triangle if sin33⁰ = a/√(a² + b²), then what is the value of [(tan²33⁰/a²) + (sin²57⁰)/b²] * sec²57⁰ * cot²33⁰?
questions.explanation: sin33⁰ = a/√(a² + b²) = perpendicular/hypotenuse ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3698/image007.jpg) = [(tan²33⁰/a²) + (sin²57⁰)/b²] * sec²57⁰ * cot²33⁰ = [(a²/b²)/a² + (b²/(a² + b²))/b²] * (a² + b²)/a² * (b²/a²) = [(1/b²) + 1/(a² + b²)] * [b²(a² + b²)/a⁴] = [(a² + b² + b²)/b²(a² + b²)] * [b²(a² + b²)/a⁴] = (a² + 2b²)/a⁴

questions.question: If 4x² - px + q is a perfect square, what is the value of (p² - 8q)/(p² + 8q)? [q≠0]
questions.explanation: For (4x² - px + q), the discriminent of the equation should be zero. D = 0 => (-p)² - 4*4*q = 0 => p² = 16q (p² - 8q)/(p² + 8q) = (16q - 8q)/(16q + 8q) = 8q/24q = ⅓ [q≠0]

questions.question: If (ax² + bx + 5) is completely divisible by (3x - 5) and (2x - 1), then what is the value of (a - b)²?
Two circles with radii 6 cm and 2 cm touch each other externally. Find the length of the direct common tangent.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3789/image017.png) OP = 6 + 2 = 8cm OC = 6 - 2 = 4 cm By Pythagoras theorem PC = √[(OP)² - (OC)²] => PC = √(8² - 4²) => PC = √(64 - 16) => PC = √48 => PC = 4√3 cm

questions.question: Solve the following expression: (1/cosA) * [sin(60⁰ + A) + sin(60⁰ - A)].
questions.explanation: = (1/cosA) * [sin(60⁰ + A) + sin(60⁰ - A)] = (1/cosA) * [(sin60⁰.cosA + cos60⁰.sinA) + (sin60⁰.cosA - cos60⁰.sinA)] = (1/cosA) * [(√3/2) * cosA + (√3/2) * cosA] = (1/cosA) * (√3cosA) = √3

questions.question: In an entrance exam, 450 girls and 600 boys appeared. If 20% of the girls and 25% of the boys are failed, find the total number of passed students.
questions.explanation: Percentage of passed girls = 100 - 20 = 80% Number of passed girls = (80/100) x 450 = 360 Percentage of passed boys = 100 - 25 = 75% Number of passed boys = (75/100) x 600 = 450 Total number of passed students = 360 + 450 = 810

questions.question: Total App users of Facebook is what per cent of total Web Users of Telegram and Instagram together?
questions.explanation: Social MediaWebAppTotal WhatsApp104050 Facebook151227 Telegram121830 Instagram82028 Total4590135 Total App users of Facebook = 12 million Total Web Users of Telegram and Instagram together = 12 + 8 = 20 million Required per cent = (12/20) * 100 = 60%

questions.question: What is the difference between total users of Instagram (both App and Web) and total App users of WhatsApp?
questions.explanation: Social MediaWebAppTotal WhatsApp104050 Facebook151227 Telegram121830 Instagram82028 Total4590135 Total users of Instagram (both App and Web) = 28 million Total App users of WhatsApp = 40 million Required difference = 40 - 28 = 12 million

questions.question: What is the average of total Web users of all the four Social media plat-forms together?
questions.explanation: Social MediaWebAppTotal WhatsApp104050 Facebook151227 Telegram121830 Instagram82028 Total4590135 Total Web users of all the four Social media plat-forms together = 45 million Required average = 45/4 = 11.25 million

questions.question: What is the ratio of total App users of WhatsApp and Instagram together to total Web users of WhatsApp, Telegram and Instagram together?
questions.explanation: Social MediaWebAppTotal WhatsApp104050 Facebook151227 Telegram121830 Instagram82028 Total4590135 Total App users of WhatsApp and Instagram together = 40 + 20 = 60 million Total Web users of WhatsApp, Telegram and Instagram together = 10 + 12 + 8 = 30 million Required ratio = 60: 30 = 2: 1

questions.question: Current ages of son and his father are in the ratio of 2:5. After 8 years the ratio becomes 1:2. What will be the ratio of ages of father and son after 10 years?
questions.explanation: Ratio of ages of father and son =2:5 Let their ages be 2x and 5x. Ratio of ages of son and father after 6 years = 1:2 (2x+8)/(5x+8)=½ →4x+16=5x+8 x=8 Therefore the age of father = 5 X 8 = 40 years Age of the son = 2 X 8 =16 years The age of father and son after 10 years will be 50 years and 26 years respectively. Subsequently the ratio of their ages will be 50:26 which is equal to 25:13

questions.question: If the compound interest on a sum for 2 years at 12½% per annum is Rs 510, the simple interest on the same sum at the same rate for the same period of time is:
questions.explanation: let the sum be 'a'. CI for two years at 12 (½)% rate = Rs. 510 so 510 = a[ (1 + 12.5/100)² - 1] on solving we get sum a = Rs 1920. so SI for same sum at same rate and same period of time = PRT/ 100 = (1920 x (25/2) x 2) x 100 = Rs. 480

questions.question: What value will come in place of question mark (?) in the following question? 256⁰.³⁴ x 256⁰.⁴¹ = ?
Evaluate: 1 +1 1 +2 2 +3 1 + ⅘
The elevation of the top of a tower from a point on the ground is 45°. On travelling 60 m from the point towards the tower the elevation of the top becomes 60°. The height of the tower in metres, is
questions.explanation: Let h be height of tower. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/8386/image079.png) Now in ΔBCD, tan 60 = CD/BC or √3 = h/x => h = x√3 ..(i) and in ΔACD, tan 45°=CD/AC 1 = h/(x+ 60) or h = x+60 From eqs (i) and (ii), we get x√3 = x + 60 x = 60/(√3 - 1) = 60(√3 + 1)/2 = 30(√3 + 1) height of tower h = x√3 = 30(3 + √3)
